content.problemStatement:
Read problem statements in RussianChef has a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. In one operation, Chef can choose one index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N) and change AiA_iAi‚Äč to AipA_i^pAip‚Äč, where p=max‚Ā°(0,‚ĆąAi2‚ĆČ‚ąí1)p = \max\left(0, {\lceil \frac{A_i}{2} \rceil} - 1\right)p=max(0,‚Ćą2Ai‚Äč‚Äč‚ĆČ‚ąí1).

Help Chef find the smallest number of operations needed to make the sum of the sequence even or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the minimum number of operations needed to make the sum of the sequence even, or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The sum of the sequence is 161616, which is already even.
Example case 2: We can change A2A_2A2‚Äč to 111, so that the sequence becomes [4,1,7,8,10][4, 1, 7, 8, 10][4,1,7,8,10]. The sum of this sequence is 303030, which is even.
Example case 3: The sum of the sequence is 181818, which is already even.
Example case 4: It is impossible to make the sum of the sequence even.
